meta.title: USBVault Enterprise Edition — Complete Knowledge Transfer
meta.purpose: Single source of truth for generating the Enterprise technical documentation. Contains the original V2.0 Fortress specification, the current Enterprise implementation state, architectural decisions, and remaining gaps.
meta.generated_by: Claude Opus 4.6 — systematic codebase analysis across 4 subsystems
meta.intended_consumer: Another Claude instance tasked with writing the Enterprise tech doc

company_mission.vision: USBVault is a portable encrypted file storage system that runs entirely from a USB flash drive. No installation. No cloud. No account. No trace.
company_mission.tagline: Carry sensitive files in your pocket, plug into any computer running Windows, macOS, or Linux, access your files with a password, and walk away leaving zero evidence you were ever there.
designed_for: Intelligence operatives and field agents requiring zero-trace portable data operations
Government and defense personnel handling sensitive or classified materials
Journalists protecting sources, investigations, and unpublished research
Legal and medical professionals carrying privileged client data
Corporate executives and security teams with confidential business intelligence
Travelers and border crossings where device inspection is a real threat
Everyday citizens who deserve the same level of data security as a spy

guiding_principles.PORTABLE: No installation. No admin for daily use. Runs entirely from USB. Any machine, any OS.
guiding_principles.SECURE: Military-grade encryption (Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305). No backdoors. Intelligence-grade by design.
guiding_principles.INVISIBLE: Hidden partitions. Hidden files. Encrypted filenames. Zero-trace cleanup. Minimal forensic footprint.
guiding_principles.RESILIENT: Crash-safe dual-index architecture. Self-healing. Append-only writes. Built for USB reality.
guiding_principles.SIMPLE: Non-technical users operate it without understanding cryptography. Double-click. Password. Done.
guiding_principles.ZERO_TRACE: No evidence of vault usage survives on the host after ejecting. Registry, prefetch, thumbnails, recent files — all cleared automatically.
guiding_principles.ZERO_TRUST: No single component is trusted with everything. Split-key architecture ensures no single system can decrypt alone.
guiding_principles.PQC_COMPLIANT: Post-Quantum Cryptography readiness. Hybrid schemes protect today's data against future quantum attacks.

PORTABLE.how: Self-hosted from TOOLS partition via companion Express service + portable Node.js + platform launchers

SECURE.how: Rust crypto core: Argon2id (64MiB,3t,4p) + XChaCha20-Poly1305 / AES-256-GCM-SIV

INVISIBLE.how: SECURE partition unmounted, chflags hidden (macOS), attrib +H+S (Windows), encrypted filenames in AEAD chunks

RESILIENT.how: Dual-index atomic commits, monotonic commit counter, state_version rollback protection, append-only VAULT.bin

SIMPLE.how: Double-click launcher → browser opens → password → done. No installation required.

ZERO_TRACE.how: zeroTraceService.js: 23 cleaners (10 Win, 5 macOS, 6 Linux), auto-clean on eject, safeEjectWithCleanup()

ZERO_TRUST.how: Wrapped MEK architecture (password→KEK→MEK). Cloud split-key (HKDF(LOCAL||REMOTE)) is V3 roadmap.

PQC_COMPLIANT.how: ML-KEM-1024 + X25519 hybrid sealed boxes in Rust, feature-gated, tested

V2.0_Universal.description: Cross-platform Python app. Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305. Zero-trace. Standalone product.

V3.0_Split_Secret.description: Cloud split-key: HKDF(LOCAL || REMOTE) = MASTER_KEY. 3-factor auth.

V4.0_Ghost.description: Steganographic embedding. Hardware key (FIDO2). Security tier selection.

enterprise_edition.description: Parallel product that takes V2.0 crypto core, rewrites in Rust, adds web/mobile UI, Go server, cloud sync, billing. Dual-mode: USB-only + Cloud-connected.

usbvault_crypto.purpose: Cryptographic core — all encryption/decryption, key derivation, vault header, streaming, PQC

usbvault_app.purpose: Cross-platform frontend — Expo Router, 37 pages, Zustand stores, crypto bridge

usbvault_server.purpose: REST API — chi router, PostgreSQL, Redis, S3, Stripe billing, WebSocket sync

usb_companion.purpose: Local USB hardware bridge — drive detection, partitioning, VAULT.bin I/O, zero-trace cleanup

pqc.v3_spec: ML-KEM-1024 + ML-DSA-87 in 16384-byte V3 header extension

streaming.nonce_derivation: base_nonce XOR chunk_index via HKDF domain separation
streaming.status: COMPLETE — enhanced over V2.0 with HKDF per-chunk keys and final HMAC

pqc.status: COMPLETE — uses hybrid approach instead of V3 header extension (see ADR-011)

key_architecture.benefit: Password change is O(1) — only re-wraps MEK, doesn't re-encrypt all data
key_architecture.fido2_integration: If FIDO2 enabled: final_key = enc_key XOR PRF_output

enterprise_v4_header.format: Sequential length-prefixed (not fixed offsets like V2)
enterprise_v4_header.backward_compat: Accepts USBVLT02, USBVLT03, USBVLT04 magic for discovery
key_differences_from_v2: 24KB instead of 4KB (room for PQC keys and wrapped MEK)
Index offsets are u32 (not u64) — sufficient for USB drives
Identity/TFA/fail-counter blocks are length-prefixed variable-length (not fixed offsets)
Verify IV is 24 bytes (not 16) for XChaCha20

tfa_credential_wire_format.spec: cred_id_len(2B u16 LE) + credential_id(var) + aaguid(16B) + label_len(1B) + label(var, max 32B)
tfa_credential_wire_format.status: COMPLETE — TfaBlock + TfaCredentialEntry with pack/unpack, 4 tests passing

partitions.name: TOOLS (custom label)
partitions.contents: Launcher scripts, portable Node.js, companion service, static web app, README, recovery guide

fifty_percent_rule.description: VAULT.bin never exceeds 50% of SECURE partition. Guarantees compaction always has room.
fifty_percent_rule.enforcement: checkCapacity() in vaultContainerService.js + appendBytes() pre-check + vaultOrchestrator.addFile() pre-check

standalone_mode.description: When static/ directory exists, companion also serves the web app UI (express.static + SPA catch-all). Enables USB-bootable deployment with zero external dependencies.

multi_vault.enterprise_status: COMPLETE — discoverVaults() scans both root VAULT.bin and vault_*/VAULT.bin subdirectories
multi_vault.identity: UUID vault_id in VAULT.bin header identity block (survives mount path changes)

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Hardware Key (FIDO2)
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: Ahead of schedule. fido2Service.ts with PRF/hmac-secret extension, combineFido2Key() XOR.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Cloud Split-Key
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: V3 roadmap. Enterprise has wrapped MEK but not HKDF(LOCAL||REMOTE).

defense_in_depth_layers.name: XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: cipher.rs + streaming.rs. Per-chunk AEAD with nonce derivation.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Memory Protection
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: memory.rs: mlock (3 platforms), Zeroizing<T>, GuardedBuffer with guard pages.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Hidden Partition
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: SECURE unmounted after provisioning. usbMounter.js mounts on demand.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Encrypted Filenames
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: Filenames in AEAD-encrypted V2RC metadata chunks. Index encrypted.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Zero-Trace Cleanup
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: zeroTraceService.js: 23 cleaners across 3 platforms. Auto-clean on eject.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: App Password + Lockout
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: appPasswordService.ts: PBKDF2-SHA256 150K iterations, 12-char min, 3 attempts, 30s lockout.

defense_in_depth_layers.name: Crash-Safe Dual-Index
defense_in_depth_layers.notes: header.rs: dual-index slots, commit counter, state_version, fsync.

module_mapping.description: How each V2.0 Python module maps to Enterprise Rust/TypeScript/Node.js

mappings.status: COMPLETE — enhanced with HKDF domain separation
DIVERGENT — email alerts via Go server, not embedded in vault
DOCUMENTED — web cannot modify filesystem timestamps

mappings.enterprise: N/A — Rust compiles to native binary

stages.name: Ghost Mode
stages.check: Re-activate if was enabled last session

enterprise.dictionary: 98,735 entries via SHA-256 bloom filter (k=10, m=1419576 bits, FPR ~0.1%)
enterprise.hibp_integration: k-anonymity API (SHA-1 prefix) with bloom filter fallback

vault_errors.description: File does not start with USBVLT0x

frontend_architecture.routing: Expo Router (file-based) — 37 tab pages + 2 auth pages

stores.name: authStore
stores.purpose: User auth, login/register, FIDO2, SRP-6a, USB standalone mode

stores.name: vaultStore
stores.purpose: File/vault management, index encryption, key hierarchy

frontend_architecture.crypto_bridge: usbvault-app/src/crypto/bridge.ts — calls Rust FFI for all crypto operations
frontend_architecture.orchestrator: usbvault-app/src/services/vaultOrchestrator.ts — coordinates Rust FFI + USB companion for vault lifecycle

dual_mode.cloud_connected: EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL set → SRP-6a server auth → cloud sync/sharing/billing
dual_mode.usb_standalone: No API URL or USB_STANDALONE_MODE=true → vault password is session credential → sessionStorage only → all features unlocked → zero-trace

server_architecture.deployment: Docker (Alpine, non-root) + Kubernetes (3 replicas, HPA, rolling updates)
server_architecture.zero_knowledge: Server NEVER handles plaintext, filenames, or encryption keys. All crypto is client-side.

original_v2_build.pipeline: 5-step: Cython compilation → String obfuscation → PyInstaller → Ed25519 signing → Verification

enterprise_build.typescript: expo export --platform web (static bundle) OR Expo EAS (iOS/Android)
enterprise_build.go: Docker multi-stage Alpine build with non-root user
enterprise_build.ci_cd: GitHub Actions: Rust/Go/TypeScript parallel jobs, cargo-audit, gosec, npm audit, Snyk
enterprise_build.deployment: Kubernetes with init container for migrations
enterprise_build.adr: ADR-013 explains why Rust replaces Cython, ADR-014 explains why build pipeline replaces Ed25519 signing

rust.coverage: Crypto roundtrips, format compat, PQC, SRP, TFA packing

go.coverage: Auth, billing, sharing, sync, middleware, BOLA, privilege escalation

auto_clean_on_eject.flow: zero-trace cleanup → unmount SECURE → eject drive
auto_clean_on_eject.restart_advisory: Displayed after eject: 'Restart your computer to clear RAM and swap'

architecture_decision_records.title: Rust crypto core
architecture_decision_records.decision: Rust FFI for all crypto (not Python/Cython)

architecture_decision_records.title: PQC Hybrid vs V3 Header
architecture_decision_records.decision: X25519+ML-KEM-1024 hybrid instead of V3 fixed PQC block

architecture_decision_records.title: Rust FFI vs Cython
architecture_decision_records.decision: Rust native compilation replaces Cython for memory safety

architecture_decision_records.title: Integrity Verification
architecture_decision_records.decision: Build pipeline (cargo-audit) replaces Ed25519 manifest signing

remaining_gaps.description: Items from the original spec NOT yet implemented in Enterprise

items.title: Cloud Split-Key (V3.0)
items.description: HKDF(LOCAL_KEY || REMOTE_KEY) = MASTER_KEY. Neither half alone can decrypt. Enterprise has wrapped MEK but not cloud split.

items.title: Steganographic Delivery (V4.0)
items.description: Vault hidden inside carrier files (images, audio). No visible VAULT.bin. Plausible deniability.

items.title: Process Isolation
items.description: V2.0 spec requires crypto in a short-lived subprocess. Enterprise runs crypto in-process via FFI.
items.mitigation: Rust's memory safety + Zeroize + mlock provides equivalent protection without process boundary

items.title: ptrace Anti-Debug
items.description: V2.0 uses ptrace(PT_TRACE_ME) and timing checks. Enterprise does device integrity checks but no ptrace.
items.mitigation: Web apps can't ptrace. Native builds could add this via Rust.

items.title: Crypto Classroom Content
items.description: V2.0 has 7 educational ciphers and 6 KDFs with brute-force demos. Enterprise has placeholder 'Coming in v1.1'.

items.title: Secure File Shredding on Host
items.description: V2.0 uses random overwrite + delete for exported files. Enterprise downloads to browser Downloads folder without shredding.
items.mitigation: Zero-trace cleanup covers temp files. Browser downloads are outside app control.
